name: Smyth Buss Co
recent_news: Founder's son Dick Smyth died unexpectedly in 2015; brother Bob Smyth acquired the company in 2017. Named a 'Great Fleet Across America' by School Bus Fleet magazine. No 2024-2026 M&A signals found.
fit_notes: Note: source record lists state as NY but company is actually Enfield, CT-based — relevant to Summit's New England footprint. Solid ~150-bus contractor with multi-district base; second-generation owner (Bob Smyth) inherited via family transition, modest succession risk over medium term. Attractive tuck-in profile.

name: Middletown Township Bus-Pro
recent_news: Could not confirm this entity as a private school bus contractor. The website 'mtbpa.com' is non-responsive. 'Middletown Township' in PA most commonly references a municipal/government entity, and the only 'bus' programs found in connection are the Middletown Township (Bucks Co.) BusPatrol school-bus camera enforcement program — not a private contractor. Address (220 Highland Ave, Media, PA) is in Delaware County. No public website, news, or business directory listing confirms a private operator at this address.
fit_notes: Likely a data-quality artifact in the source list — possibly a municipal-program record or a defunct/tiny operator. Not actionable as a Summit tuck-in without independent verification of legal entity, ownership, and contract base.

name: Suffolk Transportation Systs
districts_or_customers: Multiple Suffolk County school districts (25,000+ students/day)
recent_news: Partnered with Bay Shore SD on a federal DERA grant to deploy electric school buses (recent years). Multi-generational Corrado family leadership; multiple Corrado heirs in officer roles indicating active succession planning within the family. No public sale/M&A signals.
fit_notes: Note: source record shows only 10 employees / $337K revenue — drastically understated. This is the LARGEST private school bus operator on Long Island (1,800 buses, 2,800 employees, Suffolk County's largest transit system). Far too big to be a tuck-in — would be a platform-scale or transformational acquisition. Multiple Corrado family members active in management = limited near-term succession trigger. Strategic target rather than tuck-in.

name: All County Bus
districts_or_customers: Westchester County special-needs students (most of county)
districts from Yonkers to Peekskill (250+ routes)
Elmwood Day Camp and other summer programs
recent_news: Don Gregory spun the company off in 1998 from a family-owned Yonkers bus operator he had joined in 1984; company has grown organically and remains family-owned. Listed among NYC-area school bus operators evaluated for electric bus campaigns. No M&A or sale rumors found.
fit_notes: Strong Summit fit: Westchester-focused special-needs/SPED specialist (high-margin niche), ~170 vans, 300 employees, 250+ routes. Founder Don Gregory has been in the industry since 1984 (~40+ years) suggesting medium-term succession interest. Tucks neatly into NY metro footprint.

name: Vocell Bus Co
recent_news: Renewed 16,000 SF Malden HQ lease (reported in Boston Real Estate Times). Company originated in Billerica, MA in 1910; Goodman family has owned/operated for multiple generations. No public sale/M&A signals.
fit_notes: Excellent Summit fit: 115+ year old Boston-area family operator with diverse customer mix (public schools, universities, museums, charter) and ~100-bus fleet. Multi-generational Goodman ownership at a stage where succession may be a topic — strong tuck-in candidate for Summit's New England platform.
